title: Editas Medicine Sickle Cell Therapy Shows Breakthrough Results: 96% of Patients Event-Free in RUBY Trial
text: Editas Medicine reported breakthrough results from RUBY trial showing 96% of patients with severe sickle cell disease remained free of vaso-occlusive events following treatment with renizgamglogene autogedtemcel (reni-cel). Among 28 patients in the trial, 27 remained completely free from painful vaso-occlusive crises, the hallmark symptom of sickle cell disease. Total hemoglobin levels normalized from baseline 9.8 g/dL to 13.8 g/dL at six months, matching normal healthy levels. Fetal hemoglobin sustained above 40% in all patients from month 4 onwards, well above the anti-sickling threshold. Treatment was well-tolerated with safety profile consistent with standard stem cell transplant conditioning. All evaluable patients achieved successful engraftment within normal timeframes averaging 23 days for neutrophil recovery and 25 days for platelet recovery. Patients reported significant improvements in pain, physical function, and social activities. Results demonstrate reni-cel as potentially transformative therapy for severe sickle cell disease.
